what: The Arizona real estate salesperson exam is the state licensing test after pre-license education. It covers national principles and Arizona-specific license law for becoming a licensed salesperson.
The Georgia real estate salesperson exam licenses agents after approved coursework. It tests real estate principles plus Georgia Commission rules and license law.
The Illinois real estate broker/salesperson licensing exam (pathway-dependent) tests national content and Illinois license law administered through IDFPR’s exam vendor.
Ohio’s real estate salesperson exam is required after pre-license education to become a licensed salesperson under the Ohio Division of Real Estate & Professional Licensing.
The Pennsylvania real estate salesperson exam licenses candidates after Commission-required education, covering principles and Pennsylvania Real Estate Licensing and Registration Act topics.
The North Carolina real estate broker exam (provisional broker pathway) is administered for NCREC licensing after required education hours.
Virginia’s real estate salesperson exam is the state licensing test after DPOR-approved education, covering national and Virginia-specific law.
The Washington real estate broker exam licenses candidates through the Department of Licensing after required coursework.
Colorado’s real estate broker exam is required for licensure under the Colorado Real Estate Commission after approved education.
The New Jersey real estate salesperson exam licenses agents after Commission-required classroom hours and fingerprinting requirements.
Massachusetts salesperson licensing requires approved education and passing the state real estate exam covering principles and MA license law.
Michigan’s real estate salesperson exam is administered for LARA licensing after required pre-license education.
The CDL air brakes knowledge test covers air brake system parts, dual systems, inspections, and safe use. Drivers of air-brake vehicles typically need this knowledge test (and skills where required).
The CDL combination vehicles test covers tractor-trailer coupling, combination handling, and inspection topics needed for many Class A CDL pathways.
The doubles/triples (T) endorsement knowledge test covers pulling double or triple trailers, including coupling, handling, and inspection.
The tank vehicle (N) endorsement knowledge test covers liquid surge, baffled vs unbaffled tanks, and safe tanker driving practices.
The NCLEX-RN is the National Council Licensure Examination for Registered Nurses. Passing it is required for RN licensure in the United States after an approved nursing program.

examBody: State nurse aide / medication aide boards
what: Medication aide / medication assistant exams certify nurse aides authorized to administer certain medications in long-term care under nurse supervision. Rules vary by state.
Home health aide competency evaluations verify personal care, safety, and communication skills for aides working in client homes under a care plan.
The Registered Behavior Technician (RBT) exam from BACB certifies paraprofessionals who implement behavior-analytic services under BCBA supervision.
The AMT Registered Medical Assistant (RMA) exam certifies medical assistants in clinical and administrative domains as an alternative national MA credential.
The ASCP Medical Laboratory Technician (MLT) exam certifies laboratory technicians across core clinical lab disciplines.
Inpatient coding certifications (e.g., AHIMA CCS themes) test hospital coding knowledge including ICD-10-CM/PCS guidelines and compliance—not a substitute for official board exams.
Medical scribe competency checks cover documentation accuracy, medical terminology, EHR workflow, and HIPAA—used by training programs and employers (credentials vary).
The NREMT Advanced EMT cognitive exam certifies AEMTs with a scope between EMT and paramedic, including limited advanced airway and pharmacology.
Physical therapy aide assessments cover assisting licensed PTs/PTAs with prep, transfers, and clinic safety—scope is limited and state/employer-defined.
ASPT phlebotomy certification exams validate blood collection technique, tube handling, safety, and specimen processing for phlebotomy technicians.
The ABO basic optician exam certifies knowledge of ophthalmic optics, lenses, fitting, and related regulations for dispensing opticians.
The ACSM Certified Personal Trainer exam is an NCCA-accredited CPT credential covering assessment, programming, and exercise science.
The NSCA-CPT certifies personal trainers through the National Strength and Conditioning Association with emphasis on safe program design.
Precision Nutrition Level 1 is a nutrition coaching certification focused on behavior change and nutrition fundamentals for coaches (not an RDN credential).
The NSCA Certified Strength and Conditioning Specialist (CSCS) exam certifies professionals who design training programs for athletes.
State nail technician / manicurist written exams cover infection control, nail anatomy, and service procedures required for licensure (practical often separate).
State barber licensing written exams cover infection control, haircutting/shaving theory, chemicals, and state rules; skills exams are usually separate.
The ASWB Bachelors exam is used for BSW-level social work licensure (e.g., LSW) in many jurisdictions.
The ASWB Clinical exam is the clinical social work licensing exam used for LCSW (or equivalent) licensure in most U.S. jurisdictions.
The SHRM Certified Professional (SHRM-CP) exam certifies HR professionals on people, organization, and workplace knowledge with behavioral competencies.
The HRCI Professional in Human Resources (PHR) exam certifies operational HR knowledge including talent, employee relations, and compliance.
The PMI Certified Associate in Project Management (CAPM) exam is an entry project management certification based on PMI frameworks.
Six Sigma Green Belt exams (ASQ/IASSC-style) test DMAIC problem-solving, basic statistics, and process improvement tools.
The IRS Special Enrollment Examination (SEE) qualifies Enrolled Agents to represent taxpayers before the IRS across individuals, businesses, and representation topics.
The Series 65 Uniform Investment Adviser Law Exam qualifies candidates as investment adviser representatives in many states.
The Series 66 combines state investment adviser and agent law topics; often taken with Series 7 for dual registration pathways.
The FINRA Series 6 licenses representatives to sell mutual funds and variable products (limited securities registration).
Praxis Core Academic Skills for Educators tests reading, writing, and math skills often required for entry into teacher preparation programs.
The ParaPro Assessment measures reading, writing, and math skills for instructional paraprofessionals under ESSA-related requirements.
State unarmed security officer exams license guards to work site security. Topics typically include law, observation, emergencies, and ethics; rules vary by state.
Alcohol server / responsible beverage service exams certify bartenders and servers on ID checks, intoxication signs, and state alcohol service laws.
State notary public exams (where required) test notarial acts, identification, journals, and prohibited practices for commissioned notaries.
Forklift / powered industrial truck operator evaluations cover OSHA-aligned stability, inspection, and safe operation concepts used in employer certification programs.
OSHA 10-hour General Industry outreach training covers hazard recognition for entry-level workers. This readiness check is cognitive practice, not an OSHA Outreach card.
NATE Core is the foundational HVAC/R knowledge exam often taken with specialty NATE exams for technician certification.
ServSafe Food Handler is an entry food safety assessment for food workers covering contamination, hygiene, and time/temperature control (distinct from ServSafe Manager).

examBody: AHA / Red Cross-style first aid themes
what: First aid/CPR cognitive checks cover adult CPR/AED and common first aid emergencies. Skills cards still require in-person skills testing from an authorized provider.
The SAFE Mortgage Loan Originator Test (national component + state where required) licenses MLOs through NMLS for residential mortgage origination.
State wastewater operator certification exams (Level 1/entry) test treatment process knowledge, safety, sampling, and regulatory basics for plant operators.
Trainee/licensed appraiser exams (AQB national themes) cover USPAP concepts, valuation approaches, and appraisal reporting for state credentialing.
The National Board Exam Arts section (The Conference) is part of funeral director/embalmer credentialing pathways in many states.
The NABCEP PV Associate exam is an entry solar credential covering photovoltaic fundamentals, safety, and system applications.
Veterinary assistant assessments cover restraint, basic nursing support, hospital procedures, and safety for assistants working under veterinary supervision.
